title: Enhanced ping with 20-count default and latency graph
description:
Enhanced ping feature:
- Default to 20 pings (configurable via Settings-style row like competitor)
- Show real-time latency graph: line + area-fill chart, Y-axis in ms, sequential pings on X
- Stats summary above chart: Average, Min, Max (ms)
- Blue line with translucent area fill on dark background
- Handle first-ping spike gracefully in scale
- Reference: competitor screenshot saved at ~/.openclaw/workspace/competitor-ping.png
- Both iOS and macOS platforms
close_reason: Enhanced ping complete: latency graph, 20-count default, hardened edge cases, 46 unit tests, 9 UI tests

title: Enhanced ping UI with latency graph - both platforms
description: Implement enhanced ping with: (1) 20-count default configurable, (2) real-time latency line+area chart with Y-axis ms and sequential X, (3) stats summary (avg/min/max) above chart, (4) blue line with translucent fill on dark bg, (5) handle first-ping spike in scale. Both iOS and macOS platforms. Reference: ~/.openclaw/workspace/competitor-ping.png
close_reason: Latency graph with Charts, 20-count default, min/avg/max stats - both platforms

title: Review and harden ping implementation
description:
Review and harden the enhanced ping implementation:
1. Verify iOS PingToolViewModel handles edge cases: 0 results, all timeouts, single result
2. Verify macOS inline-state version handles same edge cases
3. Ensure chartYAxisMax handles edge cases (empty, single point, all same value)
4. Ensure ping count Picker persists selection to UserDefaults correctly
5. Verify stop/cancel mid-ping leaves clean state (no zombie tasks)
6. Both platforms should show 'No results' or empty state when pings fail completely
7. Verify accessibility identifiers are set on chart, stats card, and count picker
close_reason: Hardened: chartYAxisMax 10ms floor, pingCount UserDefaults persistence, Task.isCancelled guards, accessibility IDs on chart/stats

title: Unit tests for enhanced ping
description:
Write comprehensive unit tests for enhanced ping:
1. PingToolViewModel: test live stats computation (liveAvgLatency, liveMinLatency, liveMaxLatency)
2. PingToolViewModel: test chartYAxisMax with various distributions (normal, spike, single, empty)
3. PingToolViewModel: test successfulPings filter excludes timeouts
4. PingToolViewModel: test startPing/stopPing lifecycle with MockPingService that yields results
5. PingToolViewModel: test pingCount UserDefaults persistence
6. macOS: test ShellPingOutputParser with malformed input, empty output, partial output
7. Shared PingService: test calculateStatistics with edge cases
Target: 85%+ coverage on ping-related code. Use Swift Testing (@Test, #expect).
close_reason: 46 new unit tests: iOS VM live stats/chartYAxisMax/successfulPings/lifecycle/persistence + macOS parser edge cases

title: UI tests for enhanced ping experience
description:
Write UI tests confirming the enhanced ping experience:
1. iOS: verify chart appears after 2+ successful pings (look for pingTool_chart_latency)
2. iOS: verify live stats (min/avg/max) appear during ping execution
3. iOS: verify ping count picker changes count and persists
4. iOS: verify stats card appears after ping completes
5. macOS: verify latencyChartView appears after 2+ results
6. macOS: verify stats line (min/avg/max) in footer after completion
7. macOS: verify count picker selection works
8. Both: verify clear button removes chart and stats
Use accessibility identifiers already in the views. Add any missing ones.
close_reason: 9 new UI tests: iOS chart/stats/picker/clear + macOS chart/stats/picker/close

title: Multi-network scanning
description: Pick a router from the target screen to scan that network. Enable scanning multiple networks beyond the local one. User selects a router → app scans that router's network.
close_reason: All 5 subtasks complete: NetworkProfile model, pipeline adaptation, iOS UI, macOS UI, integration verified (106+253 tests passing)

title: NetworkProfile model and manager
description: Create NetworkProfile model (Codable, Sendable) in NetMonitorCore and NetworkProfileManager (@Observable) with: profile CRUD, active profile switching, auto-detect local network from GatewayService, UserDefaults persistence. Unit tests for all operations.
close_reason: NetworkProfile model, NetworkProfileManager, and protocol method added

title: Scan strategy and pipeline adaptation
description: Add ScanStrategy enum (.full/.remote) to NetworkScanKit. Extend ScanContext with networkProfile and scanStrategy. Add ScanPipeline.forStrategy() that returns TCP+ICMP+DNS-only pipeline for remote networks. Add IPv4Helpers.hostsInSubnet(cidr:) for generating IP lists from CIDR. Backward compatible. Unit tests.
close_reason: scanNetwork(profile:) added to protocol and DeviceDiscoveryService, makeScanTarget(profile:) routes scan to specific interface

title: iOS network switcher UI
description: iOS UI: network name badge on Dashboard, network switcher on Network Map (segmented control/dropdown), Add Network sheet with router picker and manual entry tabs, reachability validation. Wire switchProfile to trigger re-scan with new context. UI tests.
close_reason: iOS network picker added to LocalDevicesCard with menu-style Picker, DashboardViewModel gains availableNetworks/selectedNetwork state

title: macOS network switcher UI
description: macOS UI: Networks section in sidebar, network detail view, Add Network toolbar button. Refactor DeviceDiscoveryCoordinator to accept network profile. Wire to existing scan flow. UI tests.
